title: Ensure that 'Vulnerability assessment for machines'  component status is set to 'On'

input_row.title: Ensure that 'Vulnerability assessment for machines'  component status is set to 'On'
input_row.description: Enable vulnerability assessment for machines on both Azure and hybrid (Arc enabled) machines.
input_row.rationale: Vulnerability assessment for machines scans for various security-related configurations and events such as system updates, OS vulnerabilities, and endpoint protection, then produces alerts on threat and vulnerability findings.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. From Azure Home select the Portal Menu 2. Select Microsoft Defender for Cloud 3. Under Management, select Environment Settings 4. Select a subscription 5. Click on Settings & monitoring 6. Ensure that Vulnerability assessment for machines is set to On Repeat the above for any additional subscriptions. Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: 501541f7-f7e7-4cd6-868c-4190fdad3ac9 - Name: 'A vulnerability assessment solution should be enabled on your virtual machines'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. From Azure Home select the Portal Menu 2. Select Microsoft Defender for Cloud 3. Under Management, select Environment Settings 4. Select a subscription 5. Click on Settings & Monitoring 6. Set the Status of Vulnerability assessment for machines to On 7. Click Continue Repeat the above for any additional subscriptions.
input_row.impact: Microsoft Defender for Servers plan 2 licensing is required, and configuration of Azure Arc introduces complexity beyond this recommendation.
input_row.default_value: By default, Automatic provisioning of monitoring agent is set to Off.
input_row.additional_information: While this feature is generally available as of publication, it is not yet available for Azure Government tenants.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Access Azure Portal**: Navigate to Azure Home and select the Portal Menu.
2. **Locate Microsoft Defender for Cloud**: Select "Microsoft Defender for Cloud" from the menu options.
3. **Open Environment Settings**: Under the "Management" section, choose "Environment Settings."
4. **Select Subscription**: Pick the relevant subscription you want to audit.
5. **Access Settings & Monitoring**: Click on "Settings & Monitoring" within the selected subscription.
6. **Confirm Vulnerability Assessment Status**: Verify whether "Vulnerability assessment for machines" is set to "On."
7. **Repeat Steps for Additional Subscriptions**: If auditing multiple subscriptions, repeat the above steps for each.

**Validation:**
- **What to Verify**: Check if the status of the vulnerability assessment is indeed set to "On."
- **Expected Secure Configuration**: The setting should reflect that the "Vulnerability assessment for machines" is actively enabled.
- **How to Confirm Compliance**: Successful confirmation will be indicated by seeing the setting "On" in the portal for each audited subscription. 

This control requires direct access to the Azure interface and cannot be fully automated through AWS APIs, hence the manual verification approach.
